Most_Recent_Inspection: Halifax Water Meter - Request Customer Care Contact me to Arrange a Meter Installation Date
Work_Description:
Associated Building Permit: BPRES-2021-19207

The HRM Dartmouth Modular Shelter project will be constructed on the existing parking lot located at 16 Flotilla Lane in Dartmouth. The modular shelter facility will have accommodations for 26 individuals.   

The shelter will consist of four 60ft long prefabricated modular units which contain 6 individual bedrooms and 2 washroom facilities each. There will also be two 40ft modular units that will require on site modifications prior to being ready for their intended use. One of the 40ft units will be modified in order to meet barrier free requirements. It will contain two barrier free accommodations and a washroom that meets ADA standards. The other 40ft unit will be modified into a Kitchen and Security Office complete with a Staff Washroom.

The asphalt under the building footprint will be removed and the subgrade to be leveled and compacted to create a structural base. The modular units will be supported on lumber cribbing that will sit on the compacted subgrade. We will enclose the structural cribbing with an insulated skirt in order to create a chase for the Mechanical and Electrical services to run beneath the modular units.

The water and sewage connections will be brought in directly off of Alderney Drive and will stub up through the ground within our insulated chase. All services will be run above ground fastened to the underside of the modular units. The water and sanitary lines will be heat traced.

In this case that is MH#1, which falls within CN Rails active zone limits and would require extensive and costly preparations.  Some of the extraordinary costs would be for temporary shoring to prevent undermining or covering over of the tracks, engineer designed plan which would need to be submitted to CN Rail for approval, CN permit , private CN locates and a CN flagging crew – all this would add thousands of dollars to the job.

Work_Description: Tying new incoming water service to water shutoff located in right of way between street and property line. Installing new water meter in house to provide water to 3 full/1 half bath and kitchen.

Permit request is for the insurance restoration at 918 Brussels St, Halifax, due to fire damages requiring the following restoration as per scope provided.

Building is a 3 level 2 Unit Dwelling. The building has (2) civic numbers.

Unit 918 is unit that includes the the basement and main level. Fire damages affected this level only requiring the removal / gut of the interior. Plaster (Asbestos containing plaster, wood lath, and floor components were removed to wood structure.

Unit 920 is the Upper Level Unit. No structural damages to this unit requiring any removals.

Fire damages require replacement of fire damaged interior components. Due to the age of the building upgrades are being provided to suit to todays requirements and standards.


Please seee below for general scope of work.
All work to be completed per NBCC 2015 and local HRM Codes abnd Bi-Laws

Exterior / Structure
Damages to the exterior were limited to (3) windows.

Remove and replace fire damaged windows (3) in Laundry, Washroom and Lower Level
Bedroom that meets egress. No other windows were damaged at each level were damaged.
Remove 2 exterior doors at back of main level and replace with (1) double garden door. 1 of the 2 doors not required due to previous renovations completed before the fire.

Mechanical / Electrical
House was heated with hot water radiators provided through oil fired furnace. Firance has been
removed. Current heating system will be replaced with electric heat.

Supply and install new baseboard electric heat. Replaces previous oil fired furnace and hot water
Supply and install new plumbing rough-ins, pex water lines and fixtures
New electrical as per CEC Canadian Electrical Code
Smoke alarms in every Bedroom and Hallway serving a Bedroom, hardwired and interconnected
with a battery back-up
Supply and install HRV to service lower level and basement level. Chases for the duct work for
the HRV or bathroom /range hood exhaust fan will be installed on the suite side of the fire
separation. The Fire separation will be installed prior to the installation of duct chases


Interior Renovations / Restoration

Repair to fire damaged beam/ joist in Lower Level below front entrace as provided by Contrast Engineering Limited
Existing Exterior walls are 2x4” non-dimensional lumber. 2lb foam to be applied to exterior walls
at this level. Lower level walls furred out to accept R-24 Batt. Insulation to be provided to meet
Section 9.36 Div. B Part 9 NBC
To provide (1) Hr separation and a STC rating of at least 50 between Main Level and Upper Level
Unit, including (1 Hr) rating common walls at and below stairs to the second level, and load
bearing walls.
Openings to Bedrooms have been modified to suit 36” doors.
Replace Kitchen and vanities

Most_Recent_Inspection: Halifax Water Connection - Service Abandonment
Work_Description:
Please refer to DEMO-2022-00227 permit for demolition of structure located at 60 Golf Club Rd. 

From Caitlin Daly:

'This is a requirement due to the size of the service supplying the old clubhouse. Based on our records from 2003 when the new clubhouse was built, the 8” Ductile service continues past the Tee for the new clubhouse, before reducing and splitting closer to the old clubhouse. As there is no master meter on this large service at the property line, we want the abandonment of as much of the unused line as possible for two primary reasons: 1. Water siting stagnant in the unused portion of the service is a water quality risk, and 2. The unused portion of the service left in the ground is a water loss risk for un-metered water. As a result we would request abandonment take place as close as reasonably possible to the Tee supplying the new clubhouse, with a minimum distance of 1 meter. '

Work_Description:
Hello Halifax Water Team, I'd like to apply for a permit to install new services (Water and Sewer) to 18A & 18B Kennedy Drive as I would like to subdivide the lot. Currently the residential unit is a DUPLEX that I will subdivide into Semi detached. There is already a separating fire wall, both units already have separate water meters, separate heat sources (furnace) and separate electrical/power meters. In order for me to subdivide the lot, I will need to install separate WATER & WASTE Laterals into 18B Kennedy Drive. Currently, there is 2 shutoff boxes and the water meters are on individual meters inside each unit, however, they Y and tie into 1 line from the main road (See attached for your references). This will need to be separated as the water laterals need to be separate from the main road. (Kennedy Drive) I am kindly requesting to obtain a permit to allow the professional contractor Atlantic Paving ARCP to supply and install a new water and sewer lateral into the property. This will require the road to be excavated in order to supply and install a both seperate services into the property and my plumber will disconect/cap the existing Y connection for the water and sewer lines in order to seperate the services completely. Your attention to this matter is GREATLY APPRECIATED!
I have already spoken with Halifax Planning and Development, my subdivision application has been submitted SUBDIV-2024-00260 and given the green light on the subdivision. I have also already gotten clearance from 'Call before you dig' and will attach this for your records.

Most_Recent_Inspection: Halifax Water Connection - Residential Wastewater Service
Work_Description:
I'd like to apply for a permit to install new services to 56 Belle Vista Drive.
(Water and Sewer) as I would like to subdivide the lot. Currently the residential unit is a DUPLEX that I will eventually subdivide into Semi detached. There is already a separating fire wall, both units already have separate water meters, separate heat sources (furnace) and separate electrical meters. In order for me to subdivide the lot, I will need to install separate WATER & WASTE Laterals. Currently, there is only 1 stand pipe and the water meters are located in 1 of the units. This will need to be separated as the water laterals need to be separate from the main road. (Pauline Crescent) **** THERE IS CURRENTLY A WATER RENEWAL about to be taking place on the street and the roads are being torn apart by a professional company (Atlantic) this week. I need to get a permit to allow the professional contractors to trench 5' deep from the other unit to the property line so we can connect the separate water and sewer laterals. TIME IS OF THE ESSENCE therefore, your attention to this matter is GREATLY APPRECIATED!
This would be an IDEAL time for me to get this done as I have already spoken with Halifax Planning and Development and given the green light on the subdivision and I have spoken with several contractors to give me pricing to install the separate sewer and water laterals from the property to the property line.

Primary_Work_Scope: Conversion Single Unit to Multiple Units - Secondary Suite
Work_Description:
Permission is being requested to turn an existing ground floor/main level room, which has an existing walk-in closet and an existing ensuite 3 piece bathroom, into a Secondary Suite. There is an existing room above this room. One and one half of the walls of the Secondary Suite adjoin the rest of the house. The remaining walls of the Secondary Suite are exterior walls. 

The entire ceiling will be replaced with 2 layers of CGC Sheetrock (5/8 inch Firecode Core (Type X)), with acoustic sealant between layers, hung off of resilient channel, and insulated with Rockwool Safe-N-Sound insulation. 

All walls will have a layer of CGC Sheetrock (5/8 inch Firecode Core (Type X)) installed over existing drywall, with acoustic sealant between layers.

Entryway Foyer
•	The existing walk-in closet will be turned into the new Entryway Foyer.
•	Space for a new entryway door will be cut into the exterior wall and a new exterior entryway door installed.

Bathroom
•	The Bathroom will remain in the same location.
•	Existing fixtures (sink, faucets, toilet, exhaust vent) to be replaced with new ones, but the bathtub/shower unit will be replaced with a shower unit only. 
•	New washer and dryer hookups (electrical, plumbing, exhaust) will be installed. 
•	The existing window will be replaced. 
•	New vinyl flooring will be installed.

Main Room
•	One of the 2 existing windows will be replaced with one the same size. 
•	A wired-in smoke/fire detection alarm will be installed in the ceiling.
•	Additional light fixtures will be installed.
•	The space is currently heated with electric baseboard, which will remain, but a new heat pump will be installed to provide supplemental heat and air conditioning. 
•	A kitchenette will be installed and will include new sink and faucets, cabinets, countertop, fridge, electric range and over the range microwave/exhaust hood (vented outdoors). 
•	New electrical and plumbing will be installed for the kitchenette.
•	An existing interior door adjoining the Main Room to the rest of the house will be replaced with a fire-rated door.

Primary_Work_Scope: Conversion Single Unit to Multiple Units - Secondary Suite
Work_Description:
Here is a comprehensive overview of the work to be carried out in our basement to set up a secondary kitchen unit.

The project will encompass a complete overhaul of the current basement space to add a fully-functional kitchen. This work will entail plumbing installation, electrical modifications, cabinetry installation, and appliance setup.

Plumbing Installation: A new water line will be laid out to facilitate the functioning of the kitchen. This includes setting up connections for a kitchen sink, dishwasher, and potentially a laundary. All plumbing work will adhere to the necessary local and national building codes to ensure safe use.

Electrical Work: There will be a need for new electrical wiring to power kitchen appliances, such as a refrigerator, oven, microwave, and dishwasher. Additional outlets will be installed to accommodate these appliances, and a dedicated circuit will be established to avoid overloading the current electrical system. All electrical installations will comply with the local electrical code.

Cabinetry and Countertops Installation: The kitchen will be furnished with cabinetry and countertops. They will be chosen based on the available space and will provide ample storage and workspaces. They will be installed by professionals to ensure safety and longevity.

Appliance Installation: This includes the installation of essential kitchen appliances like a refrigerator, dishwasher, stove/oven, and microwave. We will ensure all appliances are installed correctly and safely, following manufacturer's guidelines.

Ventilation and Lighting: In order to maintain a healthy and safe environment, we will also install a ventilation system, likely a range hood or vent fan, to vent cooking odors and reduce humidity. We'll install appropriate lighting fixtures for task lighting, ambient lighting, and safety purposes.

Fire Safety Measures: As a safety measure, we will install a fire extinguisher in an accessible location and smoke detectors to detect any potential fire outbreaks.

This project aims to create a fully functional, safe, and efficient kitchen in the basement. All necessary permits will be obtained, and inspections will be conducted to ensure the work complies with local regulations.

Work_Description: Upgrading all plumbing from copper to plastic.

The existing Ocean Breeze Village community is being planned for redevelopment, increasing the density from approximately 140 units, to approximately 5,300 units over a 10-20 year time frame.

DesignPoint was hired to conduct capacity assessments of the existing Halifax Water infrastructure within and downstream of the existing development, to identify areas to be upgraded to accommodate the new development. In doing so, it was determined that the majority of existing Halifax Water infrastructure within the development is nearing the end of its useable service life.

Through discussion with Halifax Water, the Client, and DesignPoint, it was determined that it would be more appropriate to renew all existing Halifax Water infrastructure within the development. 

The set of plans being submitted under this application encompass all Halifax Water infrastructure upgrades which are planned to take place during 'Phase 1'. Future phases will begin as the proposed development progresses along the boundaries of the first phase of infrastructure renewal.

For more information, a detailed design memo has also been attached, which includes all water, waste water, and storm water calculations and methodology, as well as a more in depth project description.
